content.text: Model Version and SDK Radar Tracking

The Claude ecosystem releases new model versions and SDK updates on an ongoing basis. Each release may introduce new capabilities, deprecate old model strings, or change cost and latency characteristics. Enterprise teams that do not actively track these changes accumulate technical debt: hardcoded deprecated model IDs, outdated SDK versions with missing safety features, and upgrade crises when Anthropic announces end-of-life dates.

The model version you deploy today will not be the optimal choice in six months. Build version tracking into your architecture from the start rather than retrofitting it when a deprecation notice arrives.

attrs.alt: Claude Model Version Lifecycle and SDK Compatibility Matrix

content.text: Anthropic publishes Claude models as versioned strings of the form
). Each model string is immutable — the model behind a dated alias does not change — but Anthropic periodically deprecates older strings and removes them from the API.
Begin migration planning; move current model to Assess
Complete migration before date; move to Hold
Recalculate cost models; update quota forecasts

The Anthropic Python and TypeScript SDKs follow semantic versioning. Patch releases are safe to take immediately; minor releases add features but rarely break; major releases require a migration review.
Published on PyPI. Watch releases at
Published on npm. Watch releases at
Maintained separately by cloud providers; track their own release cadences in addition to the Anthropic SDK
The most common model version problem in enterprise Claude deployments is hardcoded model strings scattered across application code:
Centralise all model selections in a single configuration layer. When Anthropic deprecates a model, you change one config file, not every file in the codebase:

attrs.alt: Model Version Upgrade Pipeline with CI Gates

content.text: Subscribe to Anthropic's release signals to detect model and SDK changes automatically rather than relying on manual monitoring:
SDK Version Pinning and Dependency Policy
Enterprise applications must balance staying current (security patches, new features) with stability (avoiding surprise breaking changes). The recommended policy:
Security fixes; no breaking changes per semver
New features; verify no behavioural changes in evals
May have breaking changes; requires migration review
CVE remediation; treat as SEV2 incident

A model upgrade is not safe to deploy without verifying that performance on your specific use cases has not regressed. Build an evaluation suite specific to your application and run it as a CI gate on every model or SDK version change:

An eval pass rate below 95% on your own use-case suite should block a model version upgrade, even if the new model is superior on Anthropic's published benchmarks. Your evals measure what matters to your application.
Model versions and SDK releases map onto radar rings as follows:
Starts in Assess; trial on non-critical workloads; promote to Adopt after 2+ production pilots
Stays in Adopt while actively supported by Anthropic
Move to Assess or Trial (still works but migration needed)
Adopt after auto-merge to main and CI green
Assess until migration RFC approved; Trial during rollout; Adopt when complete
When Anthropic announces a model deprecation, follow this runbook to migrate before the end-of-support date:
Create a migration ticket. Assign an owner. Set a deadline 30 days before the Anthropic end-of-support date.
. List every integration that must be updated.
Update MODEL_CONFIG to the new model. Run the eval suite against the new model in a staging environment. Resolve any regressions.
Deploy to 5% of production traffic (canary). Monitor error rate, latency P99, and cost for 48 hours. Expand to 100% if metrics are stable.
Remove the old model string from MODEL_CONFIG. Update the radar: move the deprecated model to Hold. Add the new model to the appropriate ring.
Archive eval results and migration notes in the radar blip entry for historical reference.

Model version and SDK radar tracking transforms an inherently reactive problem — 'Anthropic deprecated the model our production system uses' — into a proactive, process-driven activity. The key practices are: centralise model string configuration, detect changes automatically via model catalog polling and Renovate/Dependabot, run a use-case-specific eval suite as a CI gate on every upgrade, and reflect model lifecycle state in the tech radar using consistent ring assignments.
Never hardcode model strings in application logic — use a central MODEL_CONFIG with feature-level assignments
Validate all configured model IDs at startup to catch deprecations before they become production incidents
Subscribe to Anthropic's model catalog API and SDK release feeds for early change detection
Gate every model upgrade on a use-case eval suite with ≥95% pass rate threshold
Assign deprecated models to Assess immediately on deprecation notice; move to Hold on end-of-support
Follow a Patch / Minor / Major SDK upgrade policy with matching automation (Renovate) and review requirements
